description: Visual Agenda PR2 - Week view with 7 stacked day strips, shared time axis, and client-side day switching. Builds on PR1 color infrastructure. Reference mock: docs/agenda-mocks/meetwhen_week_hybrid_5_plus_1.html

userStories.title: Add GetWeek method to AgendaService
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service method that returns all 7 days of agenda data in a single provider call so the week view avoids redundant fetches.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WeekView struct to internal/services/agenda.go with fields: Calendars []*models.CalendarConnection, Days [7]DayEvents, WeekStart time.Time, HostTZ *time.Location

DayEvents struct has fields: DayStart time.Time, DayEnd time.Time, Events []AgendaEvent (original unclipped events that touch this day — an overnight event 22:00 Mon to 02:00 Tue appears in BOTH Monday and Tuesday with its original Start/End preserved)
Add GetWeek(ctx, hostID string, weekStart time.Time) (*WeekView, error) method to AgendaService
GetWeek loads host and calendars once, calls AssignColors once, then calls GetAgendaEventsWithCalendars once for the full Mon 00:00 to next Mon 00:00 window
Events are assigned to each day they touch (event.Start < dayEnd AND event.End > dayStart), NOT clipped — the original Start/End are preserved so the detail list shows full duration
Each day's events are sorted by start time using slices.SortFunc
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: Events are NOT clipped to day boundaries here. Clipping happens only in FlatLane (US-002) for strip rendering. The detail list uses original event times so overnight events show their full duration. See PRD §9 table: 'Full duration in the list.'

userStories.title: Add FlatLane function for week strip blocks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that produces a single flat lane of overlaid colored blocks for one day, used in the compact week strip.
acceptanceCriteria: Add FlatLane(events []AgendaEvent, windowStart, windowEnd time.Time) []StripBlock to internal/services/agenda_strip.go
Returns all non-all-day events as StripBlocks positioned within [windowStart, windowEnd]
Events are clipped to the window boundary (same intersection logic as LanesByCalendar) — this is where clipping happens, not in GetWeek
Each block uses the event's CalendarColor (fallback #5F5E5A)
All-day events are excluded

If an event extends beyond the shared window on either side, the block is clamped to the window edge AND the block gets Overflow field set: OverflowLeft bool, OverflowRight bool (new fields on StripBlock)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Unlike LanesByCalendar (one lane per calendar), FlatLane overlays all calendars into a single lane. Overflow fields are used by the template to render small arrow indicators at the strip edge (PRD §5.2, §7.3).

userStories.title: Add OverflowLeft and OverflowRight fields to StripBlock
userStories.description: As a developer, I need overflow indicators on strip blocks so the template can show arrows for events that extend beyond the visible window.
acceptanceCriteria: Add OverflowLeft bool and OverflowRight bool fields to the StripBlock struct in internal/services/agenda_strip.go
Update LanesByCalendar to set OverflowLeft=true when an event's original start is before windowStart, OverflowRight=true when original end is after windowEnd
Update FlatLane (from US-002) to set the same overflow fields
Existing PR1 day_strip.html template continues to work (new fields are zero-value false by default)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: These fields enable the .strip-overflow arrow indicator in the template. The day strip from PR1 doesn't need overflow arrows (single-day window) but the fields won't break it.

userStories.title: Add ComputeSharedWindow for week-wide time axis
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that computes a single visible window across all 7 days so the week strip columns align.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ComputeSharedWindow(days [7]DayEvents) (time.Time, time.Time) to internal/services/agenda_strip.go
Collects all non-all-day events across all 7 days, finds the overall earliest start and latest end
Applies the same adaptive logic as ComputeVisibleWindow (baseline 09:00-18:00, extend with 30min padding, contract if narrower)
Returns times using the first day's location (all days share the same host timezone)
If no timed events in entire week, returns 09:00-18:00 baseline
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: The shared window ensures all 7 day rows have identical column alignment so hour labels at the top apply to every row.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for GetWeek, FlatLane, overflow, and ComputeSharedWindow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the new week view functions to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Test GetWeek event assignment: event 22:00 Mon to 02:00 Tue appears in BOTH Monday and Tuesday with original Start/End preserved (not clipped)
Test GetWeek: all-day event appears in the correct day only
Test FlatLane: 2 events from different calendars on the same day produce 2 blocks in one lane
Test FlatLane: all-day events excluded
Test FlatLane overflow: event starting before windowStart produces block with OverflowLeft=true
Test FlatLane overflow: event ending after windowEnd produces block with OverflowRight=true
Test ComputeSharedWindow: events only on Mon and Thu still produces a single shared window covering both
Test ComputeSharedWindow: empty week returns 09:00-18:00

Test shared-window + FlatLane integration: week with an event at 07:00 Mon and an event at 20:00 Thu produces a shared window that excludes neither, and FlatLane for a day with an event starting before the shared window produces a block with OverflowLeft=true and LeftPct=0
Build succeeds
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add tests to internal/services/agenda_strip_test.go (FlatLane, ComputeSharedWindow) and internal/services/agenda_test.go (GetWeek assignment).

userStories.title: Add WeekDayView struct for template data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a view model struct that bundles per-day strip blocks with event data so the template can render each day row and its detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WeekDayView struct to internal/services/agenda_strip.go with fields: Date time.Time, DayName string (e.g. 'Mon'), DateFormatted string (e.g. 'Apr 13'), FullDateFormatted string (e.g. 'Monday, April 13'), Blocks []StripBlock, Events []AgendaEvent, EventCount int, IsToday bool, IsActive bool
Add BuildWeekDayViews(week *WeekView, sharedWindowStart, sharedWindowEnd time.Time, today time.Time) []WeekDayView function
Function produces 7 WeekDayViews, each with FlatLane blocks positioned within the shared window
Events field contains the original unclipped events from DayEvents (for the detail panel list)
EventCount is len(Events)
IsToday is true for the day matching today's date
IsActive is true for today (or first day with events if today has none)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: FullDateFormatted and EventCount are used by the selected-day detail header (see mock line 100: 'Friday, April 17 · 8 events'). Events are unclipped originals for the detail list.

userStories.title: Add week strip and overflow CSS component styles
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CSS for the week strip rows, overflow indicators, active day highlight, and day-detail toggling.
acceptanceCriteria: Add .week-strip-container: background, border, border-radius, padding (matches mock card style)
Add .week-strip-row: grid with 90px label + 1fr lane, cursor pointer, padding 4px 0
Add .week-strip-row--active: highlighted background, left accent border, slightly more padding (see mock line 60: background + border-left 2px solid info color)
Add .week-strip-row--today: 'today' badge style (small text label next to date)
Add .week-day-details: container for all 7 pre-rendered detail blocks
Add .week-day-detail: display none by default
Add .week-day-detail--active: display block
Add .week-detail-header: flex row with day title + 'click any day above' helper text (see mock lines 99-102)
Add .strip-overflow: small arrow indicator (CSS pseudo-element or inline SVG) at strip edge for blocks with OverflowLeft/OverflowRight
Reuse existing .strip-lane, .strip-block classes from PR1
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Reference mock: docs/agenda-mocks/meetwhen_week_hybrid_5_plus_1.html. The selected row uses grid 90px+1fr, background highlight, and 2px left border.

userStories.title: Create week_strip.html partial template
userStories.description: As a host, I want to see 7 stacked day strips with a shared time axis when viewing This Week.
acceptanceCriteria: Create templates/partials/week_strip.html with {{define "week_strip.html"}}
Template accesses .Data.WeekDays ([]WeekDayView), .Data.HourLabels, .Data.Calendars
Renders a .week-strip-container with shared .strip-header (hour labels) at the top, using grid-template-columns: 90px 1fr matching the day rows
The .week-strip-container has role='tablist' attribute
Renders 7 .week-strip-row elements as <button role='tab'> (not div), each with: day label (abbreviated weekday + date), .strip-lane containing FlatLane blocks
Active day row gets .week-strip-row--active class and aria-selected='true', today row gets .week-strip-row--today with a small 'today' text label
Each row has data-day-index attribute, aria-controls='week-day-panel-{index}' linking to its detail panel
Strip blocks with OverflowLeft or OverflowRight render a .strip-overflow indicator at the corresponding edge
Calendar legend rendered inside the strip container below the rows (inside the card border, matching mock lines 89-94)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Accesses .Data because it's called via {{template "week_strip.html" .}} from the page template where . is PageData. Rows are <button role='tab'> for keyboard accessibility (Tab/Enter/Space to switch days). Mock reference: docs/agenda-mocks/meetwhen_week_hybrid_5_plus_1.html

userStories.title: Pre-render all 7 day-detail blocks in week view template
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all 7 day detail panels rendered server-side so day switching is instant with no server round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: In the week view branch of templates/pages/dashboard_agenda.html, after the week_strip partial, render a .week-day-details container
Before the details container, render a .week-detail-header with: the active day's FullDateFormatted + ' · N events' and a helper text 'click any day above' (matching mock lines 99-102)

Inside .week-day-details: iterate .Data.WeekDays using {{range $i, $day := .Data.WeekDays}} and for each day render a <div class='week-day-detail' id='week-day-panel-{{$i}}' data-day-index='{{$i}}' role='tabpanel' aria-labelledby matching the corresponding tab button>
Each detail block reuses day_detail.html partial: call {{template "day_detail.html" (dict "Events" $day.Events)}} to pass the day's events in the same shape the partial expects (.Events at top level)
The active day's div gets .week-day-detail--active class (visible), others are hidden
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Reuses day_detail.html from PR1 via dict helper to construct the expected data shape. This avoids duplicating the event list markup. The detail header is rendered once (not per-day) and updated by JS when the active day changes (US-009). dict template func already exists (added in PR1). NOTE: The week mock shows a denser table-style detail (with header row) vs PR1's simpler card-style list. PR2 accepts the PR1 detail styling for now — a week-specific detail refinement (table layout matching the mock) is deferred to a follow-up.

userStories.title: Add client-side day switching with keyboard support
userStories.description: As a host, I want to click or keyboard-navigate to a day in the week strip to instantly show that day's events below.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a small inline <script> block in dashboard_agenda.html week view section that handles day switching
Clicking a .week-strip-row: removes .week-strip-row--active and aria-selected from the previous row, adds them to the clicked row
Simultaneously: removes .week-day-detail--active from the previous detail block, adds it to the matching detail block (matched by data-day-index)
Updates the .week-detail-header text to show the newly selected day's full date and event count
Keyboard: Tab focuses the strip rows, Enter/Space activates the focused row (same as click). Arrow Up/Down moves focus between rows
No server round-trip — pure DOM class toggle
Works correctly when the page first loads (active day is pre-set server-side via IsActive)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: ~25 lines of vanilla JS. No HTMX needed for intra-week switching — the data is already in the DOM. Each row stores its full date and event count in data attributes for the header update.

userStories.title: Wire week view handler to use AgendaService.GetWeek and parse week param
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Agenda handler's week branch to use the new service method, parse the week query param, and pass the correct data to the template.
acceptanceCriteria: Update the Agenda handler's week branch in internal/handlers/dashboard.go

Parse optional ?week=YYYY-MM-DD query param; if present, normalize to Monday of that week (if the date is not a Monday, find the preceding Monday); if absent or malformed (unparseable date), fall back to current week's Monday silently (no 400 error — treat it like the param was omitted)
Call AgendaService.GetWeek with the resolved Monday
Compute shared window via ComputeSharedWindow, generate hour labels via GenerateHourLabels
Build WeekDayViews via BuildWeekDayViews
Pass to template Data map: WeekDays ([]WeekDayView), HourLabels, Calendars, WindowStart, WindowEnd — in addition to existing View, Today, Timezone fields
Remove the now-unused groupEventsByDay function and AgendaDayGroup type from dashboard.go
Existing today view branch remains unchanged
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: The ?week= param enables direct linking to a specific week (e.g. /dashboard/agenda?view=week&week=2026-04-13). PRD §6 specifies this route shape.

userStories.title: Update dashboard_agenda.html week view to use strip and detail partials
userStories.description: As a host, when I click This Week I want to see the visual week strip with clickable day rows and a detail panel below.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the existing week view branch in templates/pages/dashboard_agenda.html
New structure: {{template "week_strip.html" .}} (which includes legend internally) + detail header + pre-rendered day details (from US-008) + day switching script (from US-009)
Existing Today/This Week tab links still work
Week subtitle shows 'Week of [Monday date]' using the first WeekDayView's DateFormatted
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: This replaces the old list-based week view with the strip visualization. Calendar legend is inside week_strip.html (matching mock layout where legend is inside the strip card).
